message:
fix: critical image.js load-time throw breaks text embedding in standalone binary

The sharp patch made sharp=null when unavailable, but image.js has:
  if (BROWSER_ENV) { ... }
  else if (sharp) { ... }
  else { throw new Error('Unable to load image processing library.'); }

In a standalone binary (not browser, no sharp), this throws at module
load time, crashing @xenova/transformers entirely — even for text-only
embedding pipelines that never use image processing.

Fix: replace the hard throw with deferred stubs for loadImageFunction,
createCanvasFunction, and ImageDataClass. These only throw if image
processing is actually attempted, not at import time. Text embedding
(feature-extraction pipeline) never calls these functions.

Also: added FORCE_COLOR=0 to CLI subprocess env (some tools ignore
NO_COLOR when FORCE_COLOR is set), and added defensive .catch() on
the orphaned Promise.all in the timeout race pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
